title: Matricaria subnivalis
taxonomy: Matricaria subnivalis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Matricaria, which includes several species of daisies and other related plants. Matricaria subnivalis is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant is typically found growing in areas with well-drained soil and full sun. Its classification reflects its close relationship to other members of the Asteraceae family, which includes many common and important plant species.
uses: Matricaria subnivalis, also known as the alpine marigold, is a plant species with limited information available on its medicinal uses. However, in general, the Matricaria genus is known for its potential health benefits. Some of the medicinal uses of Matricaria species include treating digestive issues, soothing inflammation, and relieving anxiety. Further research is needed to confirm the specific medicinal uses of Matricaria subnivalis. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using any plant-based remedy.
benefits: Matricaria subnivalis is a plant species that is known for its potential health benefits. It contains compounds that may have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which could contribute to its therapeutic uses. The plant's extracts have been studied for their ability to protect against cell damage, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Additionally, Matricaria subnivalis may have antimicrobial properties that could help combat infections and promote a healthy gut microbiome. Further research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of Matricaria subnivalis and its potential applications in medicine.
properties: Matricaria subnivalis is a plant species known to possess therapeutic properties. Its extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating various health issues. The plant's bioactive compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, are thought to be responsible for its therapeutic properties. Research suggests that Matricaria subnivalis may have antimicrobial, antiviral, and antifungal properties, making it a promising natural remedy. Further studies are needed to fully understand the plant's therapeutic potential and its safety for human use.
constituents: Matricaria subnivalis contains flavonoids and phenolic acids as its active constituents, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant also contains sesquiterpene lactones, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Additionally, Matricaria subnivalis contains essential oils, such as chamazulene and farnesene, that provide further medicinal benefits. These compounds work together to promote the plant's therapeutic effects. The exact composition and proportions of these active constituents may vary depending on the plant's growing conditions and harvesting methods.

intro: Matricaria subnivalis, also known as subalpine chamomile, is a small annual plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) that grows in high-altitude mountainous regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, such as insomnia, anxiety, and digestive problems, due to its calming and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Matricaria subnivalis include apigenin, luteolin, and chamazulene, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Matricaria subnivalis is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or essential oil, and is also used in skincare products and as a food additive due to its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties.
